nodes.title: Single pnpm monorepo for @trembus roblox-ts packages
nodes.excerpt: Fifteen reusable roblox-ts libraries (logging, persistence, stats, inventory, timers, UI, …) needed a single home with one toolchain. The code predates its git history — the init commit 0a5572a (2026-05-01) is annotated…

nodes.title: Packages are framework-agnostic (no Flamework imports)
nodes.excerpt: Consuming games differ in architecture: soul-steel-official uses Flamework DI; other consumers (and any future Luau consumer) do not. A package that imports Flamework forces that framework onto every consumer.

nodes.title: Peer-dependency consumption model
nodes.excerpt: Internal @trembus/ and common @rbxts/ libraries must not be bundled twice into a consuming game — duplicate module instances break singleton state (signals, registries) in the Roblox DataModel.

nodes.title: One concern per package gate
nodes.excerpt: Package monorepos accrete grab-bag "utils" packages unless there is a gate at creation time. Once a junk-drawer package exists, everything leaks into it.

nodes.title: Open-taxonomy rebuild of slotable-items
nodes.excerpt: @trembus/slotable-items hard-codes its item-category taxonomy. Adding a category or item kind means editing the package itself — every game's inventory needs fork the platform. This violates the open/closed grain the re…

nodes.title: Adopt Project-System for this space
nodes.excerpt: The monorepo had planning signal without a planning layer: CLAUDE.md and two SOP/overview docs, plus a cited-but-nonexistent "ADR 0005". The previous Roblox-side Project-System consumer (Soul-Steel-Official) de-migrated…

nodes.title: Dual-lane TypeScript and Luau package strategy

add-a-package.title: Add a package
add-a-package.caption: A new @trembus package scaffolded, built, consumer-linked, and recorded.

steps.label: One-concern proposal gate
steps.detail: State the single concern; if the proposal needs an 'and', it is two packages
steps.note: Decision 0004. Variants (Flamework / Rojo / React / internal deps) are declared here too.

steps.label: Scaffold packages/<name>
steps.detail: src/, package.json, tsconfig.json; main out/init.lua, types out/index.d.ts

steps.label: Wire dependencies
steps.detail: Peers + workspace:* dev mirrors; peerDependenciesMeta for optional integrations
steps.note: Decision 0003 — never direct dependencies for shared libs.

steps.label: pnpm --filter @trembus/<name> build
steps.detail: rbxtsc emits out/

steps.label: Consumer link: smoke test
steps.detail: Add link: dep in roblox-testing-environment and build it

steps.label: Regenerate packages registry
steps.detail: node tools/build-packages-registry.mjs (this space)

concept-to-studio-architectural-build.title: Concept-to-Studio architectural build
concept-to-studio-architectural-build.caption: A seed concept becomes a selected architectural system, constructible Roblox manifest, and verified Edit-mode Studio blockout.

steps.label: State the seed concept
steps.detail: Intent, fantasy, essential experience, and any non-negotiable image or spatial idea
steps.note: The seed may be rough. Do not ask it to solve dimensions, materials, or Roblox implementation yet.

steps.label: Refine the design contract
steps.detail: Users, site, scale, parti, rooms, connectors, mood, constraints, and success criteria
steps.note: Create one pipeline entity for the run and one canonical creative bundle. Keep the concept runtime-neutral until the owner gate.

steps.label: Generate three distinct concepts
steps.detail: A / B / C vary governing parti, mass-to-void relationship, circulation, and structural expression
steps.note: They must not be palette swaps. Shared brief constraints remain fixed so the comparison is meaningful.

steps.label: Select or synthesize a direction
steps.detail: Approve one direction or explicitly combine named features
steps.note: Reject returns to refinement. No section diagrams or build manifest are produced before this gate closes.

steps.label: Decompose into unique sections
steps.detail: Site, exterior masses, rooms, connectors, thresholds, service zones, and exceptional details
steps.note: Each section receives a frontmatter Markdown record with stable IDs, dimensions in studs, adjacency, materials, asset references, and acceptance checks.

steps.label: Draw architectural diagrams
steps.detail: Only the views each section needs: plan, section, elevation, axonometric, circulation, or connection detail
steps.note: Use a consistent grid, orientation, player scale, diagram legend, and conceptual-versus-measured label.

steps.label: Compile the Roblox build manifest
steps.detail: Stud grid, assemblies, Parts, materials, assets, connectors, lighting, budgets, tags, and build order
steps.note: Separate existing registered assets, local-only assets, generated candidates, primitives, and placeholders. Built-in Roblox materials are preferred before custom assets.

steps.label: Choose owner and target place
steps.detail: Project, TGL domain, target experience, staging root, and intended runtime handoff
steps.note: Only this gate authorizes project-specific paths or TGL names. Room, corridor, and zone candidates map to LAY_ROM, LAY_COR, and LAY_ZON after ownership is selected.

steps.label: Resolve asset readiness
steps.detail: Exact path, registry state, checksum, TGL name/status, and fallback for every non-primitive dependency
steps.note: Support art has no runtime suffix. Runtime candidates default to BLK; BLK is local proof and is not upload-ready.

steps.label: Preflight the selected Studio
steps.detail: get state → list Studios → confirm/set active → require Edit DataModel → inspect staging root
steps.note: Write a concise GenerationManifest with bundle path/hash, selected concept, BLK status, builder version, and promotion_authorized=false.

steps.label: Build from the manifest
steps.detail: Deterministic Parts and assemblies first; insert exact assets; generate only scoped missing candidates
steps.note: Persist only in Edit mode. Build reversibly under an owned lab root; do not mutate an unrelated place or source lab.

steps.label: Verify architecture and playability
steps.detail: Hierarchy, anchoring, collision, traversal, sightlines, scale, materials, missing assets, budgets, and screenshots
steps.note: Use Edit audit plus disposable Play-mode checks. A failed check returns to the section, diagram, or manifest that owns the error.

steps.label: Persist proof and evidence
steps.detail: Save place snapshot → syncback dry-run → apply → review diff; manual Save to File for RBXM when required
steps.note: Studio MCP can construct and validate the DataModel but does not replace the explicit snapshot/syncback or RBXM export handoff.

steps.label: Declare the next lifecycle state
steps.detail: Remain prototype/BLK, mark proven, or authorize a separate production promotion
steps.note: Creation does not silently promote, upload, publish, or choose a production package.

ui-component-promotion.title: UI component promotion
ui-component-promotion.caption: A lab prototype becomes a cataloged, proven, productized component with FNL assets.

steps.label: Build kit in lab place
steps.detail: Studio MCP, free-form; GenerationManifest required (TGL status BLK)
steps.note: Masters in ReplicatedStorage.UIMockups; previews deployed to StarterGui. No uploads at this stage.

steps.label: Register catalog entry
steps.detail: Attributes: Component, State=prototype, SourceGuiPath, Variants
steps.note: UIStudio.Catalog entry + template instance; the in-place ledger both agents maintain (contract v1 in decision 0008).

steps.label: Prove in lab rigs
steps.detail: Icon / tile-state / motion labs; set State=proven
steps.note: The labs are the QA rigs: pixel rulers, tint/state/fallback probes, reduced-motion checks, texture galleries.

steps.label: Syncback to roblox-labs
steps.detail: rojo syncback; git history begins

steps.label: Promote assets BLK→FNL
steps.detail: Open Cloud upload at FNL only; record rbxassetid in manifest

steps.label: Choose production lane
steps.detail: rbx-ui React (ts) vs instance-template + Luau controller (luau)
steps.note: First Luau-lane promotion triggers the M4 lane scaffold per decision 0007.

steps.label: Implement package in mono
steps.detail: State=promoted; catalog entry links the package

root.label: Roblox-development-studio
root.brief: A first-principles, project-agnostic framework for the planning layer of any project. One contract — ProjectEntity — that every planning artifact derives from, plus the zero-dependency tooling that validates, scaffolds, guards, and renders it. This guide is itself derived from the contract: the framework anatomy below is authored, the _project/ surface and conventions are generated live from this project's config, so the guide can't drift from reality.

children.label: .project-system/ — the vendored core
children.brief: How a consuming project carries the framework: vendor schema/ + lib/ + tools/ verbatim into a reserved .project-system/ at the project root, and never edit or rename a file inside it — updating means re-copying the folder. The only project-specific file an adopter writes is project-system.config.json at the root. This core is universal: identical in every project.

children.label: schema/
children.brief: The contract layer — machine-readable JSON Schema. Holds the universal base contract every consumer mirrors, and the meta-schema that validates a project's config.

children.label: project-entity.base.schema.json
children.brief: The universal contract: the three primitives plus the link/rel definitions. Identical across every consuming project — the artifact a consumer mirrors, and what the drift check compares against.

children.label: project-config.schema.json
children.brief: The meta-schema: validates a project's project-system.config.json. The only project-specific file an adopter authors is checked against this.

children.label: lib/
children.brief: The shared library every engine reads through — a zero-dependency parser plus the seam that composes the base schema with a project's config. Single source: no tool re-implements either.

children.label: md.mjs
children.brief: Zero-dependency frontmatter/markdown parser — the single source all tools read frontmatter and body sections through, so they can never drift on what a file says.

children.label: contract.mjs
children.brief: The seam between the project-agnostic engines and a project's specifics. loadContract() composes base schema + config into the runtime `ctx` every engine consumes; loadEntities() reads the _project/ tree, deriving kind from the folder and id from the filename.

children.label: tools/
children.brief: The zero-dependency engines, all reading the one contract via lib/contract.mjs — no check is re-implemented in a second place. The validator is the single check the others reuse.

children.label: validate.mjs
children.brief: The validator + validateEntity — the single check (per-kind enums, link resolution, prose ↔ frontmatter) that the scaffolder, guard, and renderer all reuse.

children.label: new-entity.mjs
children.brief: The scaffolder behind the single /new <kind> command. Validates the kind against your config and writes valid frontmatter + the kind's scaffold sections, so there are no per-kind commands to hand-maintain.

children.label: guard.mjs
children.brief: The PreToolUse guard — the only hook that can block. Rejects any _project/ write that would break the contract (exit 2 + reason); read-only and fails open.

children.label: render-hub.mjs
children.brief: The renderer. Projects the validated entity graph into the two JSON contracts the Command Center consumes (graph.json + hub.json) — including this very guide. Domain-neutral end to end.

children.label: check-consumer-drift.mjs
children.brief: The packaging discipline: asserts each consumer mirrors the canonical contract (schema, validator behavior, and hook parity). The gate that keeps the un-published mirror honest.

children.label: the two hooks
children.brief: The contract is enforced by exactly two Claude Code hooks: a BLOCKING PreToolUse(Write|Edit) guard (tools/guard.mjs — the only hook that can block; exit 2 + reason, read-only, fails open) and an ADVISORY SessionStart health summary (validate.mjs --summary, always exit 0). Rendering is NOT a hook — it's the Command Center's Vite dev plugin. Consumers vendor these verbatim.

children.label: _project/ — planning surface
children.brief: The project's planning surface — every file here is exactly one ProjectEntity. There is one subfolder per declared kind, and the subtree below is generated from THIS project's project-system.config.json, so it reflects exactly the kinds, folders, naming schemes, and status enums this project declares. kind is derived from the folder; id from the filename; the authored surface of a file is just title · status · updated (+ optional links · tags).

children.label: decisions/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "decision". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the serial · 4-digit scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "proposed" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: 0001-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a decision (synthesized from the serial · 4-digit scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: reports/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "report". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "draft" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: YYYY-MM-DD-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a report (synthesized from the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: pipeline/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "pipeline". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "design" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a pipeline (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: roadmap/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "roadmap". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "proposed" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a roadmap (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: sessions/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "session". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "planned" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: YYYY-MM-DD-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a session (synthesized from the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: workflows/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "workflow". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "draft" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a workflow (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: packages/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "package". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "proposed" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a package (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: The three primitives
children.brief: Everything reduces to three primitives — Identity (kind · id · title) · State (status · updated) · Relation (links[]). kind and id are loader-derived from the file's path; the rest is the authored frontmatter. Everything else a file might carry is an optional `tags` facet. A good reduction deletes fields; this one dropped many per-kind interfaces to one shape.

children.label: Relation vocabulary
children.brief: The universal vocabulary for the typed edges between entities (links[].rel), defined once in the base schema and shared by every project. WHICH kinds each rel may point at is this project's config (relTargetKinds) — the facts below are that resolution. A link target is a `<folder>/<id>` path, a milestone marker, or an external ref.
